title: Heatwave cool-room plan
summary: A room-level plan for reducing heat exposure when air conditioning is unavailable, weak, or interrupted.
content: Choose the coolest room by observation, not preference. Block sun before walls heat up, reduce midday movement, group vulnerable people near check-ins, schedule water reminders, and set a destination if the room becomes unsafe. Heat danger is a medical risk, not just discomfort.
steps: Find the coolest room at the hottest hour.
List infants, elderly people, sick people, and heavy workers for checks.
Move heavy tasks to safer hours.
Plan a cooler destination if the room fails.
warnings: Confusion, fainting, very hot skin, worsening weakness, or inability to drink needs urgent help.
Fans may not be enough in extreme heat.
Never leave children, elderly people, or pets in parked vehicles.
common_mistakes: Opening windows to hotter noon air without checking.
Waiting for someone to complain before checking.
Doing heavy chores because the schedule says so.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Heatwave cool-room plan with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Heatwave cool-room plan if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Heatwave cool-room plan and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Heatwave cool-room plan or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Heatwave cool-room plan are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Heatwave cool-room plan”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Heatwave cool-room plan as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Heatwave cool-room plan when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Heatwave cool-room plan.
short_term: For the first hours of Heatwave cool-room plan, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Heatwave cool-room plan remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Heatwave cool-room plan fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Heatwave cool-room plan to the real environment: check water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
